title: actinodaphne johorensis
intro: Actinodaphne johorensis, also known as Johore Actinodaphne, is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. It is a shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Actinodaphne johorensis is often used as a tincture or tea, and it can also be dried and made into a powder for consumption or used in topical applications.
